engineIntelligence.h2: Rolls Royce Phantom Engines - Codes, Common Failures & Replacement Costs
engineIntelligence.description: The cards below cover the three V12 petrol engines fitted to the Rolls Royce Phantom in the UK, including documented failure causes, variant production years, and typical rebuilt engine costs based on current UK market data. All pricing reflects supply-only rebuilt units from specialist UK rebuilders.

cards.altText: Rolls Royce Phantom N73B68 engine - petrol 6.75L replacement UK
knownFailures: Valve stem seal wear - oil consumption increases after 60k miles on high-mileage units.
Timing chain guide degradation - plastic guides wear causing rattle, typically 70k-100k miles.
VANOS solenoid drift - variable valve timing faults causing rough idle or misfires.

cards.altText: Rolls Royce Phantom N74B66 engine - petrol 6.6L replacement UK
productionHistory: Phantom VII Series II Standard Wheelbase (RR2) - 2012-2017
Phantom VII Series II Extended Wheelbase (RR2) - 2012-2017
knownFailures: Turbo wastegate actuator fatigue - rattle or boost loss, typically 50k-80k miles.
Oil feed line coking - heat degradation causing turbo oil leaks on sustained load.
Electronic water pump controller failure - cooling system faults triggering limp mode.

cards.altText: Rolls Royce Phantom N74B68 engine - petrol 6.75L replacement UK
productionHistory: Phantom VIII Series II Extended Wheelbase (RR2) - 2023-2025
knownFailures: Direct injection HPFP wear - high-pressure pump degradation causing misfires after 60k miles.
Otto particle filter saturation - emissions compliance issues on urban-driven examples.
Valvetronic III recalibration drift - stop-start system faults requiring software update.
cards.replacementCost: Typical UK rebuilt engine: £19,000-£21,000 (supply only) Estimated from comparable N74 units - actual quotes may vary.

engineGuide.h2: Rolls Royce Phantom Engine Codes - Full Technical Specifications & Replacement Guide
engineGuide.h3: Every engine code fitted to the Rolls Royce Phantom across all generations, with technical specs, compatible variants and trims, known failure points, and average rebuilt prices from UK specialists. Select any engine code to compare quotes.

entries.title: 6.75L Petrol
entries.history: Signature Phantom VII V12 delivering effortless torque; defines ultra-luxury refinement but requires proactive timing chain maintenance.
compatibleVariants: Standard Wheelbase, Extended Wheelbase, Drophead Coupé, Coupé, Series I, Series II
commonFailures: Valve stem seal wear causing oil consumption after 60k miles - documented across Phantom VII fleet.
Timing chain guide degradation leading to rattle at 70k-100k miles - preventative replacement recommended.
VANOS solenoid drift causing rough idle or misfires - updated parts available per RR-07-12.
entries.cta: Get quotes for N73B68 Rolls Royce Phantom engine replacement

entries.title: 6.75L Petrol
entries.history: Early production N73 variant with initial calibration; shares core architecture with N73B68 but requires specific ECU pairing.
commonFailures: Early valve stem seal material prone to hardening - oil consumption evident by 50k miles.
First-gen VANOS units more susceptible to solenoid clogging - service interval critical.
entries.cta: Get quotes for N73B68A Rolls Royce Phantom engine replacement

entries.title: 6.75L Petrol
entries.history: Revised N73 variant with updated emissions calibration; improved reliability over early units but retains core failure patterns.
compatibleVariants: Standard Wheelbase (Series II), Extended Wheelbase (Series II), Drophead Coupé (late), Coupé
commonFailures: Revised valve stem seals extend life but still wear by 70k miles - oil consumption remains key indicator.
Updated timing chain guides more durable but still require inspection at 80k miles.
entries.cta: Get quotes for N73B68B Rolls Royce Phantom engine replacement

entries.title: 6.75L Petrol Direct Injection
entries.history: Series II N73 with direct injection technology; improved efficiency but introduces HPFP and carbon buildup considerations.
compatibleVariants: Standard Wheelbase Series II, Extended Wheelbase Series II, Drophead Coupé Series II, Coupé
commonFailures: High-pressure fuel pump wear causing misfires after 60k miles - documented in FSA-RR1-09.
Carbon buildup on intake valves due to direct injection - requires walnut blasting service.
entries.cta: Get quotes for N73B675 Rolls Royce Phantom engine replacement

entries.title: 6.6L Petrol Twin-Turbo
entries.history: Phantom VII Series II and VIII twin-turbo V12; delivers refined power with improved emissions but introduces turbo-specific maintenance.
compatibleVariants: Phantom VII Series II SWB/EWB, Phantom VIII SWB, Black Badge (early)
commonFailures: Turbo wastegate actuator fatigue causing rattle or boost loss at 50k-80k miles - upgraded parts available.
Oil feed line coking from heat soak - leads to turbo oil leaks on sustained high-load driving.
Electronic water pump controller failure triggering cooling faults - distinct from mechanical pump issues.
entries.cta: Get quotes for N74B66 Rolls Royce Phantom engine replacement

entries.title: 6.6L Petrol Twin-Turbo
entries.history: Early N74B66 variant with initial turbo calibration; shares architecture with later units but requires specific wastegate actuator pairing.
compatibleVariants: Phantom VII Series II SWB/EWB (early production)
commonFailures: First-gen wastegate actuators more prone to premature wear - rattle evident by 45k miles.
Oil feed line material less heat-resistant - coking issues appear earlier than later revisions.
entries.cta: Get quotes for N74B66A Rolls Royce Phantom engine replacement

entries.title: 6.6L Petrol Twin-Turbo
entries.history: Revised N74B66 with updated emissions calibration; improved turbo durability but retains core failure patterns requiring proactive maintenance.
compatibleVariants: Phantom VII Series II SWB/EWB (late production), Phantom VIII SWB (early)
commonFailures: Revised wastegate actuators more durable but still require inspection at 60k miles.
Updated oil feed lines reduce coking risk but heat management remains critical for longevity.
entries.cta: Get quotes for N74B66B Rolls Royce Phantom engine replacement

entries.title: 6.6L Petrol Twin-Turbo Technical Update
entries.history: High-output N74 variant for Black Badge and Zenith editions; reinforced internals handle increased torque but require specialist rebuilding.
compatibleVariants: Phantom VIII Black Badge, Zenith Edition, EWB Zenith
commonFailures: High-output turbochargers experience increased stress - wastegate actuator wear accelerated under performance use.
Reinforced oil pump gears essential - standard gears prone to failure under Black Badge torque loads.
Valvetronic III recalibration drift affecting stop-start - requires specialist software update post-rebuild.
entries.cta: Get quotes for N74B66TU Rolls Royce Phantom engine replacement

entries.title: 6.75L Petrol Twin-Turbo
entries.history: Phantom VIII Extended Wheelbase exclusive V12; combines N74 architecture with increased displacement for ultimate refinement and torque delivery.
compatibleVariants: Phantom VIII Extended Wheelbase, Phantom VIII Series II Extended Wheelbase
commonFailures: Direct injection HPFP wear causing misfires after 60k miles - revised pump design improves longevity.
Otto particle filter saturation on urban-driven examples - emissions compliance requires specialist cleaning.
Valvetronic III system recalibration drift - stop-start faults resolved with updated software mapping.
entries.cta: Get quotes for N74B68 Rolls Royce Phantom engine replacement

entries.title: 6.75L Petrol Twin-Turbo
entries.history: Early N74B68 variant with initial calibration; shares core architecture with later units but requires specific ECU pairing for Phantom VIII EWB.
commonFailures: First-gen HPFP units more prone to wear - misfire codes appear earlier than revised pumps.
Initial Otto particle filter calibration less robust - urban driving accelerates saturation.
entries.cta: Get quotes for N74B68A Rolls Royce Phantom engine replacement

entries.title: 6.75L Petrol Twin-Turbo
entries.history: Revised N74B68 with updated emissions and performance mapping; improved reliability but retains core failure patterns requiring proactive maintenance.
compatibleVariants: Phantom VIII Series II Extended Wheelbase
commonFailures: Revised HPFP units extend service life but still require inspection at 70k miles.
Updated Otto particle filter management reduces saturation risk but urban driving remains a factor.
entries.cta: Get quotes for N74B68B Rolls Royce Phantom engine replacement

commonProblems.h2: Rolls Royce Phantom Engine Problems - What It Costs to Fix & When Replacement Makes Sense
commonProblems.h3: The Rolls Royce Phantom's V12 engines-N73B68 naturally aspirated and N74B66/N74B68 twin-turbo-are engineered for refinement but face specific failure patterns driving replacement enquiries. Timing chain guide wear on N73 units and turbo wastegate fatigue on N74 engines dominate UK search volume. Every rebuilt engine supplied through EngineMarket includes upgraded internal components designed to address the original failure point-plus a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty.

problems.h4: Phantom V12 Timing Chain Failure - Why N73B68 Engines Fail Prematurely
problems.affectedModels: Rolls Royce Phantom VII Standard/Extended Wheelbase, Drophead Coupé, Coupé (2003-2017, N73B68, N73B68A, N73B68B)
problems.rootCause: Plastic timing chain guides degrade from heat cycling and oil contamination, causing chain slack, rattle, and eventual jump.

repairOptions.tier: Minor - Preventative Guide Replacement - Replace guides/tensioner before failure
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove front cover, replace plastic guides and hydraulic tensioner with updated parts
repairOptions.longevity: Viable only if caught early; prevents catastrophic valve damage but labour-intensive

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Rebuilt N73B68 Engine - Supply & fit rebuilt unit with upgraded components
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove failed engine, install rebuilt N73B68 with metal-reinforced guides, bench-tested, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Recommended once rattle is audible; addresses root cause and restores factory refinement

problems.recommendation: On a Phantom VII worth £80,000-£150,000, preventative timing chain service at 60k miles offers better value than waiting for failure. If rattle is present, replacement with a rebuilt engine including upgraded guides is the only reliable long-term solution.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt N73B68 engine prices for your Rolls Royce Phantom

problems.h4: Phantom Engine Oil Consumption Excessive - N73B68 Valve Stem Seal Failure
problems.affectedModels: Rolls Royce Phantom VII all variants (2003-2017, N73B68 family)
problems.rootCause: Valve stem seals harden and crack from heat cycles, allowing oil to seep into combustion chambers causing blue smoke and increased consumption.

repairOptions.tier: Intermediate - Valve Stem Seal Replacement - Replace seals without full head removal
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Use pneumatic seal replacement tool via spark plug holes; replace all 24 seals with updated material
repairOptions.longevity: Effective if cylinder bores are undamaged; may not resolve consumption if rings are worn

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Rebuilt N73B68 Engine - Supply & fit with upgraded seals and rings
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Install rebuilt engine with revised valve stem seals and piston rings; bench-tested, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Recommended if consumption exceeds 1L/1,000 miles or compression is low; addresses multiple wear points

problems.recommendation: For Phantoms under 70k miles with good compression, seal replacement offers cost-effective resolution. If consumption is severe or mileage exceeds 80k, a rebuilt engine with upgraded seals and rings provides better long-term value and warranty protection.
problems.cta: Find reconditioned N73B68 engines with updated valve seals for your Phantom

problems.h4: Phantom Twin-Turbo Wastegate Failure - N74B66/N74B68 Boost Loss
problems.affectedModels: Rolls Royce Phantom VII Series II, Phantom VIII (2012-2025, N74B66, N74B68, N74B66TU)
problems.rootCause: Wastegate actuator diaphragms fatigue from constant boost pressure cycling, causing rattle, boost leaks, and reduced power output.

repairOptions.tier: Minor - Actuator Replacement Only - Replace faulty wastegate actuators
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove turbochargers, replace actuators with upgraded units, recalibrate boost mapping
repairOptions.longevity: Viable if turbo cores are undamaged; addresses rattle but doesn't prevent future core wear

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Rebuilt N74 Engine - Supply & fit with upgraded turbos and actuators
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Install rebuilt N74 engine with reinforced turbochargers and actuators; bench-tested, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Recommended if boost loss is severe or mileage exceeds 60k; addresses turbo core wear and actuator fatigue

problems.recommendation: On Phantom VIII models worth £200,000+, actuator replacement offers good value if caught early. If boost loss is accompanied by turbo shaft play or oil consumption, full engine replacement with upgraded turbos provides comprehensive resolution and warranty coverage.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt N74B66 turbo engine prices for your Rolls Royce Phantom

problems.h4: Phantom Electronic Water Pump Failure - N74 Cooling System Faults
problems.affectedModels: Rolls Royce Phantom VII Series II, Phantom VIII (2012-2025, N74B66, N74B68)
problems.rootCause: Electronic controller circuitry fails from heat exposure in tight engine bay, causing pump shutdown, overheating warnings, and limp mode activation.

repairOptions.tier: Intermediate - Pump & Controller Replacement - Replace complete water pump assembly
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Drain coolant, remove failed pump/controller unit, install revised assembly with updated wiring
repairOptions.longevity: Effective if no secondary damage occurred; revised units have improved heat shielding

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Rebuilt N74 Engine - Supply & fit with upgraded cooling components
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Install rebuilt engine with revised water pump, thermostat housing, and coolant lines; bench-tested
repairOptions.longevity: Recommended if overheating caused secondary damage; addresses multiple cooling system wear points

problems.recommendation: For Phantoms with isolated pump failure and no overheating history, pump replacement offers cost-effective resolution. If temperature warnings were ignored or coolant loss occurred, rebuilt engine replacement prevents repeat failures and includes comprehensive cooling system upgrades.
problems.cta: Get quotes for N74 cooling system rebuilds for your Rolls Royce Phantom

problems.h4: Phantom Valvetronic System Failure - N73/N74 Variable Valve Lift Faults
problems.affectedModels: Rolls Royce Phantom all variants (2003-2025, N73B68, N74B66, N74B68)
problems.rootCause: Eccentric shaft sensor drift or actuator motor wear causes incorrect valve lift calibration, triggering power loss, rough idle, and limp mode.

repairOptions.tier: Intermediate - Sensor/Actuator Replacement - Replace faulty Valvetronic components
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove valve cover, replace eccentric shaft sensor and/or actuator motor, recalibrate via ISTA
repairOptions.longevity: Viable if mechanical components are undamaged; requires specialist coding equipment

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Rebuilt Engine with Updated Valvetronic - Supply & fit rebuilt unit
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Install rebuilt engine with revised Valvetronic components and updated software mapping; bench-tested
repairOptions.longevity: Recommended if mechanical wear is present or recalibration fails repeatedly; addresses root cause comprehensively

problems.recommendation: For isolated sensor faults with good mechanical condition, component replacement offers good value. If Valvetronic errors persist after recalibration or mechanical wear is evident, rebuilt engine replacement with updated components provides reliable long-term resolution.
problems.cta: Find rebuilt Phantom engines with updated Valvetronic systems

problems.h4: Phantom High-Pressure Fuel Pump Failure - N73B675/N74 Direct Injection Issues
problems.affectedModels: Rolls Royce Phantom Series II, Phantom VIII (2009-2025, N73B675, N74B66, N74B68)
problems.rootCause: HPFP internal components wear from high-pressure cycling, causing fuel pressure loss, misfires, hard starting, and potential lean-condition damage.

repairOptions.tier: Intermediate - HPFP Replacement Only - Replace high-pressure fuel pump assembly
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove failed pump, install revised unit with updated internal components, recalibrate fuel mapping
repairOptions.longevity: Effective if no secondary damage occurred; revised pumps have improved durability

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Rebuilt Engine with Updated Fuel System - Supply & fit rebuilt unit
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Install rebuilt engine with revised HPFP, injectors, and fuel rails; bench-tested, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Recommended if misfires caused catalytic damage or mileage exceeds 70k; addresses multiple fuel system wear points

problems.recommendation: For isolated HPFP failure with good compression and no misfire history, pump replacement offers cost-effective resolution. If misfires caused secondary damage or fuel trim adaptations are extreme, rebuilt engine replacement with updated fuel components provides comprehensive resolution and warranty protection.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt direct injection Phantom engine prices

items.title: Rolls Royce Phantom 6.6L Petrol Engines
items.description: The 6.6L twin-turbo V12 (N74B66) powered Phantom VII Series II and early Phantom VIII models, delivering refined boost-assisted torque while meeting stricter emissions standards than the naturally aspirated N73. This displacement represents the transition to forced induction within the Phantom lineage, maintaining signature silence through bespoke turbocharger mounting and exhaust isolation.
commonFailurePoints: Turbo wastegate actuator fatigue at 50k-80k miles; oil feed line coking from heat soak

Both displacements prioritise cabin silence through unique mounting, exhaust routing and calibration distinct from BMW applications.
commonFailurePoints: N73 valve stem seal wear at 50k-80k miles; N74 HPFP wear
Otto particle filter saturation on urban-driven examples

faq.h2: Rolls Royce Phantom Engine Replacement - Frequently Asked Questions

items.question: How much does a Rolls Royce Phantom engine replacement cost in the UK?
items.answer: Rebuilt Rolls Royce Phantom engines typically cost £14,000-£21,000 supply-only depending on engine code, with N73B68 units at the lower end and N74B68 twin-turbo at the premium end. Supply & fit adds £2,500-£4,500 depending on location and variant complexity.
keyPoints: N73B68 naturally aspirated rebuilt: £14,000-£15,500 supply-only
N74B66/N74B68 twin-turbo rebuilt: £18,000-£21,000 supply-only
Supply & fit installation adds £2,500-£4,500 to engine price
All rebuilt units include 12-month unlimited mileage warranty from UK specialists
items.cta: Compare rebuilt Rolls Royce Phantom engine prices from vetted UK specialists

items.question: What engine codes fit my Rolls Royce Phantom?
items.answer: Phantom VII (2003-2017) uses N73B68 family naturally aspirated V12 codes; Phantom VIII (2017-present) uses N74B66/N74B68 twin-turbo V12 codes. Exact fitment depends on chassis code (RR1/RR2), body style and production year.
keyPoints: Phantom VII RR1: N73B68, N73B68A, N73B68B, N73B675 (6.75L NA V12)
Phantom VII Series II/Phantom VIII RR2: N74B66, N74B68 (6.6L/6.75L twin-turbo V12)
Black Badge/Zenith: N74B66TU high-output calibration variant
Enter registration above for instant DVLA-verified engine code matching
items.cta: Verify your Rolls Royce Phantom engine code compatibility instantly

items.question: How long does a Phantom engine replacement take in the UK?
items.answer: Typical turnaround is 3-5 working days for rebuilt engine supply, plus 2-3 days for specialist installation if choosing supply & fit. Expedited 48-hour delivery available for urgent requirements on common N73B68 units.
keyPoints: Rebuilt engine build time: 3-5 working days from order confirmation
Nationwide delivery: 1-2 working days via specialist pallet transport
Specialist installation: 2-3 working days at vetted UK fitment centres
Total project time: 5-10 working days from quote acceptance to road-ready
items.cta: Get your Rolls Royce Phantom engine replacement timeline quote today

items.question: Is a reconditioned Phantom engine reliable?
items.answer: Yes-when reconditioned by Phantom specialists using updated components addressing known failure points like N73 valve seals or N74 turbo actuators, with bench testing and 12-month warranty coverage.
keyPoints: Reconditioned units replace wear items per Rolls-Royce service bulletins
Bench-tested to factory idle stability and compression specifications
12-month unlimited mileage warranty on all reconditioned components
Source only from specialists with Phantom-specific rebuild experience
items.cta: Find reconditioned Rolls Royce Phantom engines with specialist warranty coverage

items.question: Can I use a used Phantom engine from salvage?
items.answer: Yes-if sourced from verified low-mileage UK chauffeur fleets with full service history, compression-tested and borescope-inspected pre-purchase to confirm internal condition.
keyPoints: Used Phantom engines: £4,200-£8,200 depending on mileage and condition
Require pre-purchase compression test and borescope inspection report
Limited 3-6 month warranty typical; ideal for short-term ownership or resale prep
Verify donor vehicle had no accident, flood or fire damage history
items.cta: Check verified used Rolls Royce Phantom engine availability with inspection reports

items.question: What causes Phantom V12 timing chain failure?
items.answer: N73B68 timing chain failure stems from plastic guide degradation due to heat cycling and oil contamination, causing chain slack, audible rattle and potential valve damage if ignored.
keyPoints: Plastic timing chain guides degrade at 70k-100k miles on N73B68 engines
Audible rattle at idle is the primary early warning symptom
Preventative replacement with metal-reinforced guides recommended at 60k miles
Rebuilt engines include upgraded guides addressing the root cause
items.cta: Compare rebuilt N73B68 engines with upgraded timing components for your Phantom

items.question: Why does my Phantom engine consume excessive oil?
items.answer: Excessive oil consumption in N73B68 Phantom engines typically results from valve stem seal hardening and cracking, allowing oil to seep into combustion chambers causing blue smoke on startup.
keyPoints: Valve stem seals harden from heat cycles, evident by 50k-80k miles
Blue smoke on cold startup is the primary diagnostic indicator
Seal replacement viable if cylinder bores show minimal wear
Rebuilt engines include revised seal material and piston ring upgrades
items.cta: Find Rolls Royce Phantom engines with updated valve stem seals and rings

items.question: Are Phantom VII and VIII engines interchangeable?
items.answer: No-Phantom VII (RR1) N73 engines and Phantom VIII (RR2) N74 engines differ in mounting points, ECU calibration, ancillary routing and exhaust configuration, preventing direct interchangeability.
keyPoints: RR1 chassis uses N73B68 family; RR2 chassis uses N74B66/N74B68 family
Different engine mounts, wiring harness pinouts and exhaust manifold routing
ECU software calibration unique to each generation and variant
Cross-generation swaps require extensive re-engineering and are not recommended
items.cta: Verify your Rolls Royce Phantom chassis code for exact engine compatibility

items.answer: Yes-EngineMarket connects you with vetted UK Phantom specialists offering nationwide delivery and supply & fit installation at accredited centres across England, Scotland and Wales.
keyPoints: Nationwide pallet delivery with lift-gate service to your location
Supply & fit available at specialist centres in London, Manchester, Birmingham and more
Pre-shipment compression and leak-down reports provided for transparency
Single-point warranty coverage for engine and installation when choosing supply & fit

items.answer: All rebuilt and reconditioned Phantom engines supplied through EngineMarket include a minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty covering internal components, turbochargers and ancillaries against mechanical failure.

Covers timing components, oil pump, fuel system and turbochargers
Excludes damage from improper installation or pre-existing vehicle faults
Always confirm exact warranty terms with your chosen supplier before purchase
